meanings.definition: an illustration that is drawn by hand and published in a book, magazine, or newspaper
examples: it is shown by the drawing in Fig. 7

meanings.definition: a representation of forms or objects on a surface by means of lines
examples: he did complicated pen-and-ink drawings like medieval miniatures

meanings.definition: the creation of artistic pictures or diagrams
examples: he learned drawing from his father

meanings.definition: players buy (or are given) chances and prizes are distributed by casting lots
act of getting or draining something such as electricity or a liquid from a source
examples: the drawing of water from the well

meanings.definition: the act of moving a load by drawing or pulling
cause to move by pulling
get or derive
examples: He drew great benefits from his membership in the association

meanings.definition: make a mark or lines on a surface
examples: trace the outline of a figure in the sand

meanings.definition: make, formulate, or derive in the mind
examples: What do you make of his remarks?

meanings.definition: bring, take, or pull out of a container or from under a cover
examples: The mugger pulled a knife on his victim

meanings.definition: represent by making a drawing of, as with a pencil, chalk, etc. on a surface
take liquid out of a container or well
examples: She drew water from the barrel

meanings.definition: give a description of
examples: He drew an elaborate plan of attack

meanings.definition: select or take in from a given group or region
examples: The participants in the experiment were drawn from a representative population

meanings.definition: elicit responses, such as objections, criticism, applause, etc.
examples: The President's comments drew sharp criticism from the Republicans
The comedian drew a lot of laughter

meanings.definition: suck in or take (air)
move or go steadily or gradually
examples: The ship drew near the shore

meanings.definition: remove (a commodity) from (a supply source)
examples: The doctors drew medical supplies from the hospital's emergency bank

meanings.definition: choose at random
earn or achieve a base by being walked by the pitcher
examples: He drew a base on balls

meanings.definition: bring or lead someone to a certain action or condition
examples: The President refused to be drawn into delivering an ultimatum
The session was drawn to a close

meanings.definition: cause to flow
write a legal document or paper
examples: The deed was drawn in the lawyer's office

meanings.definition: engage in drawing
examples: He spent the day drawing in the garden

meanings.definition: move or pull so as to cover or uncover something
allow a draft
require a specified depth for floating
pull (a person) apart with four horses tied to his extremities, so as to execute him
examples: in the old days, people were drawn and quartered for certain crimes

meanings.definition: cause to move in a certain direction by exerting a force upon, either physically or in an abstract sense
examples: A declining dollar pulled down the export figures for the last quarter

meanings.definition: take in, also metaphorically
examples: She drew strength from the minister's words

meanings.definition: direct toward itself or oneself by means of some psychological power or physical attributes
examples: Her good looks attract the stares of many men
The ad pulled in many potential customers
The store owner was happy that the ad drew in many new customers

meanings.definition: thread on or as if on a string
examples: the child drew glass beads on a string

meanings.definition: stretch back a bowstring (on an archer's bow)
examples: The archers were drawing their bows

meanings.definition: pass over, across, or through
examples: He ran his eyes over her body
She ran her fingers along the carved figurine
He drew her hair through his fingers

meanings.definition: finish a game with an equal number of points, goals, etc.
contract
examples: The material drew after it was washed in hot water

meanings.definition: reduce the diameter of (a wire or metal rod) by pulling it through a die
steep; pass through a strainer
remove the entrails of
flatten, stretch, or mold metal or glass, by rolling or by pulling it through a die or by stretching
cause to localize at one point
